description: Agilent 3499A/B/C Switch/Control System mainframe. High-density/high-speed switching and digital I/O for plug-in modules (MUX, GP relay, matrix, DIO, multifunction, RF/microwave/optical). Programmable via SCPI over GPIB, LAN, or RS-232. 3499A has slots 0-5, 3499B has slots 0-2, 3499C has slots 0-9 (slot 0 is the control board/built-in DIO). Operates in SCPI mode or HP 3488A emulation mode.

identity.description: Returns the instrument identification string containing the manufacturer, model number, serial number, and firmware revision.

commands.name: close_channels
commands.description: Closes the switching channels specified in the channel list. Cannot be used to change bits on a digital I/O module. Channels may not close simultaneously; the order is not guaranteed.

parameters.name: channel_list
parameters.range: Form @snn (s=slot, nn=channel). Single (@snn), list (@snn,snn), range (@snn:snn), grouped ranges (@snn:snn,snn:snn), or any combination.

commands.name: close_state_query
commands.description: Returns a comma-delineated list of all closed channel relays on all installed plug-in modules, each in the form snn (slot/channel). Reflects programmed state only.

commands.name: open_channels
commands.description: Opens the switching channels specified in the channel list, or ALL switching channels. The query returns 1 for open and 0 for closed for each channel in the list.

parameters.name: channel_list
parameters.range: Form @snn; single, list, range, grouped ranges, or combination; or the keyword ALL to open all channels.

commands.name: card_pair
commands.description: Groups two cards of the same type so a command sent to one executes on both (lowest slot first). Useful for 4-wire scanning. Specify -1 as the second slot to cancel the pair. Query returns four comma-separated slot numbers (0,0,0,0 if none paired).

parameters.name: slot2
parameters.range: Slot of identical module, or -1 to cancel pairing

commands.name: function_mode
commands.description: Configures the function mode of an N2260A or N2266A configurable MUX module: 80-ch 1-wire, 40-ch 2-wire, dual 20-ch 2-wire, or 20-ch 4-wire. Changing mode opens all channels on the module. Query returns WIRE1, WIRE2, BIWIRE2, or WIRE4.

commands.name: diag_speek
commands.description: Reads an 8-bit data register on the 44475A Breadboard Module. Returns a decimal binary-weighted value of the DI lines. Register 4 is the DI register. Generates an error on newer N22xxx modules.

commands.name: diag_spoke
commands.description: Writes an 8-bit decimal value to a register on the 44475A Breadboard Module DO lines. Register 0 is the DO register. Generates an error on newer N22xxx modules.

commands.name: scan_list
commands.description: Creates a scan list of switch channels, digital bits, and/or stored channel setups (up to 200 entries). DIO lines in the list must use handshake Mode #1 or #2. Query returns the channel sequence in list order.

parameters.name: scan_list
parameters.range: Form @snn; single, list, range, grouped ranges, or combination. May include stored setups. Up to 200 entries.

commands.name: scan_clear
commands.description: Clears all channels/bits/stored setups from the scan list. Does not affect scan configuration parameters.

commands.name: scan_size_query
commands.description: Returns the number of entries in the scan list as an integer between 0 and 200. A stored setup counts as one channel.

commands.name: arm_source
commands.description: Specifies the event control source for the arm layer (sweep-to-sweep). Default after power-on/reset is IMMediate. Query returns the source string.

parameters.name: source
parameters.range: BUS, EXTernal, IMMediate (default), TIMer, MIX, HOLD

commands.name: arm_count
commands.description: Sets the number of times the scan list is performed. MIN=1 sweep, MAX=99999 sweeps, INFinity continues until ABORt. Default after power-on/reset is 1 (MIN).

parameters.name: number
parameters.range: 1 to 99999, or MIN (1), MAX (99999), INFinity (-1 returned)

commands.name: arm_timer
commands.description: Sets the sweep-to-sweep interval in the arm layer. Valid only when ARM:SOURce is TIMer. Default after power-on is 0 (MIN).

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Specifies the event control source for the trigger layer (channel-to-channel advance). Default after power-on/reset is IMMediate. Query returns the source string.

parameters.name: source
parameters.range: BUS, EXTernal, IMMediate (default), TIMer, MIX, HOLD

commands.name: trigger_timer
commands.description: Sets the channel-to-channel interval in the trigger layer. Valid only when TRIGger:SOURce is TIMer. Default after power-on is 0 (MIN).

commands.name: channel_delay
commands.description: Sets a delay between a scan-list channel closing and the next operation (and trigger-out pulse, if configured). Can be set per channel or for ALL channels. Default after power-on/reset is 0.

parameters.name: channel_list
parameters.range: Form @snn; single, list, range, grouped, combination; or ALL

commands.name: config_ext_trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the location (slot) of the external trigger input lines. Built-in trigger lines are on slot 0; a 44474A DIO module can supply EI/CC lines on its slot. Default after power-on/reset is 0.

commands.name: config_ext_trigger_output
commands.description: Enables/disables the external trigger-out pulse output after a channel closes during a scan. Default after power-on/reset is OFF. Query returns 0 (disabled) or 1 (enabled).

commands.name: initiate
commands.description: Takes the instrument out of the idle state and moves it to the arm layer. Must always be sent to start a scan regardless of arm/trigger sources.

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Stops a scan in progress regardless of trigger source. Does not affect scan configuration or scan list. The last-scanned channel remains closed; a scan cannot resume from where interrupted.

commands.name: trigger_immediate
commands.description: Software trigger used as the trigger source in the arm or trigger layer when SOURce is set to HOLD.

commands.name: trigger_bus
commands.description: IEEE 488.2 common command used as the trigger source in the arm or trigger layer when SOURce is set to BUS.

commands.name: dig_mode
commands.description: Sets the digital I/O handshake mode (1-5) for a port. Mode 1 (static, default) and 2 (read what was written) support scan lists and card pairing; modes 3, 4, 5 do not. Only the first port on a module can use modes 3-5. Not valid for the built-in 4-bit DIO. Query returns 1-5.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: 1 (static #1, default), 2 (static #2), 3 (read or write strobe), 4 (read and write strobe), 5 (full handshake)

commands.name: dig_control_polarity
commands.description: Sets the polarity of the PCTL (peripheral control) handshake line for a slot. Not valid for the built-in 4-bit DIO (slot 0). Default after power-on/reset is POSitive. Query returns POS or NEG.

commands.name: dig_flag_polarity
commands.description: Sets the polarity of the PFLG (peripheral flag) handshake line for a slot. Not valid for the built-in 4-bit DIO. Default after power-on/reset is POSitive. Query returns POS or NEG.

commands.name: dig_io_polarity
commands.description: Sets the polarity of the I/O direction handshake line for a slot. Not valid for the built-in 4-bit DIO. Default after power-on/reset is POSitive. Query returns POS or NEG.

commands.name: dig_data_polarity
commands.description: Sets the data-line polarity for a digital I/O port. Does not change the current data line state. Default after power-on/reset is POS. Query returns POS or NEG.

parameters.name: port
parameters.range: Form snn (slot + first channel of port). 090 = built-in 4-bit port.

commands.name: dig_read_bit
commands.description: Reads a single bit from the specified bit_port and returns 0 or 1. Built-in 4-bit DIO bits are 091-094.

parameters.name: bit_port
parameters.range: Form snn (slot + bit). Built-in: 091-094. Plug-in: module dependent.

commands.name: dig_read_value
commands.description: Reads data from the specified port as a binary-weighted integer. BYTE=8 bits (0-255), WORD=16 bits (-32768..32767, 2's complement), LWORD=32 bits (2's complement). BYTE assumed if width omitted.

parameters.name: port
parameters.range: Form snn (slot + first channel). 090 = built-in 4-bit port.

commands.name: dig_read_block
commands.description: Returns block data in the form <#digits><length><block> from the specified port. Block may contain 1 to 2048 bytes. Set port to 090 for the built-in 4-bit port. BYTE assumed if width omitted.

commands.name: dig_read_trace
commands.description: Reads a block of data from a digital I/O port into a previously-defined system memory block. BYTE assumed if width omitted.

parameters.name: sys_mem_name
parameters.range: Name of a defined memory block (up to 12 chars)

commands.name: dig_read_trace_data
commands.description: Returns a block of data from the specified system memory location in the form <#digits><length><block> (block 1-2048 bytes).

parameters.name: sys_mem_name
parameters.range: Name of a defined memory block

commands.name: dig_write_bit
commands.description: Writes a 0 or 1 to the specified bit_port. Built-in 4-bit DIO bits are 091-094.

commands.name: dig_write_value
commands.description: Writes a decimal value (bit pattern) to the specified port. BYTE=8 bits (0-255; built-in 090 = 0-15), WORD=16 bits (2's complement), LWORD=32 bits (32-bit modules only). BYTE assumed if width omitted.

commands.name: dig_write_block
commands.description: Writes block data in the form <#digits><length><block> to the specified port (block 1-2048 bytes). Set port to 090 for the built-in 4-bit port. BYTE assumed if width omitted.

commands.name: dig_write_trace
commands.description: Writes a previously-stored memory block to the specified digital I/O port. The memory must have been defined with TRACe:DEFine and loaded with TRACe[:DATA].

parameters.name: sys_mem_name
parameters.range: Name of a defined and loaded memory block

commands.name: dig_trace_define
commands.description: Defines a named system memory block for digital data. Two blocks may be defined; total size must not exceed 32768 bytes. Optional fill value initializes every byte. Query returns the block size in bytes.

commands.name: dig_trace_catalog
commands.description: Returns a string containing the name(s) of defined memory blocks, separated by a comma if two are defined. (Also documented as DEFine:CATalog? in the functional grouping.)

commands.name: dig_trace_data
commands.description: Loads a previously-defined memory block with block data in the form <#digits><length><block> (block 1-2048 bytes).

parameters.name: sys_mem_name
parameters.range: Name of a previously-defined memory block

commands.name: dig_trace_delete
commands.description: Removes a single previously-defined memory block by name.

parameters.name: sys_mem_name
parameters.range: Name of a defined memory block

commands.name: dig_trace_delete_all
commands.description: Removes all defined digital memory blocks.

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: IEEE 488.2 common command. Saves the current instrument setup in the specified memory location. A saved location can be used as a channel in a scan list. Locations 01-10 (firmware 1.0/2.0/3.0) or 01-50 (firmware 4.0).

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: IEEE 488.2 common command. Restores the instrument to a previously saved instrument state.

commands.name: state_delete
commands.description: Deletes a single stored instrument state or all stored states.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears all event registers and the Status Byte register and the error queue. Does not affect enable registers.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Sets/queries the Standard Event Status Enable register (mask determining which event bits set the ESB bit 5 of the Status Byte). Query returns a decimal-weighted value.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Queries the Standard Event Register and returns its decimal-weighted value. Bits: 0=Power On, 2=Command Error, 3=Execution Error, 4=Device Error, 5=Query Error, 7=Operation Complete.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Sets/queries the Service Request Enable register (mask determining which Status Byte bits assert RQS, bit 6). Query returns a decimal-weighted value.

commands.name: status_byte
commands.description: Reads the Status Byte register and returns a decimal-weighted summary of its bits. Bit 4 = MAV (data in output buffer), bit 7 = OPR summary.

commands.name: status_operation_condition
commands.description: Queries the Operation Status Condition register (real-time). Bit 0 = waiting in trigger layer, bit 1 = waiting in arm layer, bit 4 = scan started.

commands.name: status_operation_event
commands.description: Queries the Operation Status Event register, returning the binary-weighted sum of latched event bits. Reading clears the register.

commands.name: status_operation_enable
commands.description: Sets/queries the Operation Status Enable register (mask determining which event bits set the OPR summary bit 7 of the Status Byte). Applicable bit values are 1 (trigger layer), 2 (arm layer), 16 (scan started). Query returns a decimal-weighted value.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Clears (sets to 0) all Operation Status Enable register bits. Does not affect other register groups or the event register.

commands.name: identify
commands.description: IEEE 488.2 common command. Returns the identification string: manufacturer, model number, serial number, and firmware revision.

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Reads the error queue (FIFO, up to 10 entries). Returns error number and message, or just the number depending on environment. Empty queue returns +0,"No error". Overflow yields -350,"Queue overflow".

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Returns a string indicating the SCPI version implemented on the mainframe control board, e.g. "Version A.02.00".

commands.name: system_card_type
commands.description: Returns a string with the module identification and serial number in the specified slot (e.g. "40CH MUX N2260A, Serial #"). Returns "NO CARD 00000" for an empty slot.

commands.name: relay_cycles_query
commands.description: Returns the relay cycle count for the specified channel(s), stored in non-volatile module memory. Returns -1 for channels/modules that cannot respond. Multiple channels return comma-separated integers in list order.

parameters.name: channel_list
parameters.range: Form @snn; single, list, range, grouped, combination. May include tree relays s98/s99 on N2260A/N2266A.

commands.name: relay_cycles_max
commands.description: Returns the maximum relay cycle count among all relays on the module in the specified slot. Does not indicate which channel.

parameters.name: slot
parameters.range: Slot of a supported switching module

commands.name: relay_cycles_clear
commands.description: Resets the relay cycle counter of the specified channel(s) to zero. Use when replacing a relay.

parameters.name: channel_list
parameters.range: Form @snn; single, list, range, grouped, combination

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: IEEE 488.2 common command. Sets the Operation Complete bit in the Standard Event Register when all prior commands finish executing. The *OPC? query returns +1 when commands have completed (useful for synchronizing without the status system).

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Resets the instrument and returns parameters to default settings. Clears system memory, restores default digital configuration, and clears the error queue.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Begins a self-test and returns a status code: +0 all passed, +1 ROM failed, +2 GPIB failed, +3 RS-232 failed, +4 front-panel failed. Takes several seconds and holds the bus until complete.

commands.name: wait
commands.description: IEEE 488.2 common command. Prevents the instrument from executing further commands until all prior overlapped commands have completed.

commands.name: card_power_on
commands.description: Resets a specific module (or ALL modules) to its power-on state: opens all switching channels or sets all DIO ports to inputs. Does not affect card pairing or scan configuration, and does not reset the mainframe.

commands.name: display_message
commands.description: Writes a message (up to 13 characters) to the front-panel display. Only upper-case is shown; trailing special characters (, . : ;) do not count toward the 13.

commands.name: display_state
commands.description: Turns the front-panel display on or off. ADRS, RMT, and ERROR annunciators may still light when off. Default after power-on/reset is on. Query returns 0 (off) or 1 (on).

commands.name: monitor
commands.description: Enables monitor mode on the front-panel for a slot, channel, or port. Send -1 to disable. Only one slot/channel/port can be monitored at a time. Built-in DIO: monitor port 090 or bits 091-094. Tree relays s98/s99 cannot be monitored. Query returns the monitored item or -1.

parameters.name: target
parameters.range: Slot, channel (snn), or port; -1 to disable

commands.name: system_mode
commands.description: Selects the instrument system mode: SCPI or HP 3488A emulation. Changing the mode resets the instrument (RS-232/GPIB settings preserved); allow at least 5 seconds before other operations. Query returns "SCPI" or "HP3488A".

commands.name: system_local
commands.description: Sets the instrument to local mode for RS-232 operation. All front-panel keys are fully functional.

commands.name: system_remote
commands.description: Sets the instrument to remote mode for RS-232 operation. All front-panel keys except Local, View, Mon, Enter, the knob, and the arrow keys are disabled.

commands.name: system_rwlock
commands.description: Sets the instrument to remote mode with all front-panel keys disabled (remote with lockout). Same as SYSTem:REMote except all keys are locked.
